---
title: "ABLE/529 Hourly Run Log"
format: gfm
author: "Cursor Automation"
date: 2026-03-09
---

## Run: 2026-03-09T19:02:23Z (cron)

### Objective
Advance ABLE/529 expertise with a durable QMD knowledge workflow and prioritized task system.

### Actions completed

1. Launched specialized research agents for:
   - ABLE federal rule baseline.
   - 529 federal baseline (including rollover pathways).
   - ABLE/529 interaction risks and planning tradeoffs.
2. Launched a targeted fact-check agent for volatile/uncertain claims.
3. Created persistent QMD knowledge artifacts:
   - `00-tasklist.qmd`
   - `10-core-knowledge.qmd`
   - `99-run-log.qmd`
4. Structured knowledge using confidence labels (High/Medium) and an uncertainty register.

### Strategy decision for next runs

- **Primary strategy:** uncertainty-first compounding.
  1. Close the highest-impact ambiguity (ABLE-to-Work status/limits).
  2. Build section-level citation index for 529/529A/4973 to reduce interpretation drift.
  3. Build a state conformity tracker to convert federal knowledge into execution-safe advice.

### Specialized inquiries used

- ABLE federal rules researcher.
- 529 federal rules researcher.
- ABLE/529 interactions researcher.
- Federal claim fact-checker.

### Risks and controls

- Risk: relying on secondary summaries for fast-moving legislation.
  - Control: prioritize IRS/SSA/Congress primary sources; mark unresolved points as uncertain.
- Risk: state tax recapture/conformity differences causing bad recommendations.
  - Control: never finalize state-specific advice without state-level confirmation.

### Carry-forward checklist (next run)

- [ ] Resolve ABLE-to-Work post-2025 status using current-year IRS forms/instructions.
- [ ] Build hard-citation index for IRC 529, 529A, 4973 and key effective dates.
- [ ] Start state tracker with top 3 high-population deduction states.
